fix.description: ROOT CAUSE FOUND (not the SHA-1 — you added that correctly). The release build runs minifyEnabled=true but the ProGuard/R8 rules kept only Capacitor classes, so R8 obfuscated the Google Credential Manager classes (GetGoogleIdOption, GoogleIdTokenCredential, androidx.credentials). Play Services then could not vend a verifiable ID-token credential and Credential Manager returned status 16 ('[16] Account reauth failed'). This explains everything: it failed only on alpha/release (never debug), on BOTH sign-in styles, and was unaffected by the correct SHA-1. FIX (android/app/proguard-rules.pro, a44b7a26): added R8 keep rules for googleid + androidx.credentials(+playservices) + gms.auth.api.identity. VERIFIED in the v140 release mapping.txt — GetGoogleIdOption + the credential classes are now un-renamed (no longer stripped). Built + uploaded to Play ALPHA as v140 (1.6.82). TO TEST: update from the Play closed-testing (alpha) track to v140 and sign in with a fresh Gmail. TWO musts: (1) install FROM THE PLAY TRACK — not a sideloaded/locally-built APK; Play re-signs with the App-Signing cert A5:83:5C (the one registered + in google-services.json), while a sideloaded upload-key APK (B9:5C:EA) is on no OAuth client and will always [16]. (2) If it STILL fails on v140, in Cloud Console -> OAuth consent screen confirm User type = External (Internal/Workspace-only [16]s every personal Gmail regardless of SHA). Diagnosed via a 4-agent investigation workflow.

previousFix.description: Root cause of '[16] Account reauth failed': on Android, @capgo/capacitor-social-login routes through Credential Manager, which defaults filterByAuthorizedAccounts:true — it ONLY offers Google accounts that have ALREADY authorized this app. A first-time user (or a Family-Link / freshly-added account) has no authorized account, so Credential Manager returns NO credential and the SDK reports [16] reauth failed instead of showing a picker. Fix (v134, src/lib/google-auth.ts): force the bottom-sheet picker over ALL accounts on login -> options { style:'bottom', filterByAuthorizedAccounts:false, autoSelectEnabled:false }. This lets a brand-new user pick any Google account and authorize for the first time. tsc clean; shipped in versionCode 134 (1.6.76), built + uploaded to Play alpha. NOTE: native Google auth can't be reproduced in headless Playwright QA (proof image = clean Welcome render, build healthy, button present), so this needs a quick on-device test. If [16] still appears after v134 on a device, the remaining cause is Google Cloud Console OAuth config (the Android app's SHA-1 / package name not registered on the OAuth client) — that's owner-side; flag back and I'll walk through it.

judge.note: Code fix is solid + tsc-clean and the build renders cleanly (0 console errors), but the native Google account-picker cannot be exercised in headless QA, so on-device confirmation is still owner-side.
